entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Wild and cultivated sorghum, specifically Sorghum bicolor

entities.name: COMPOSITE GERMPLASM SET
entities.description: A set of circa 3000 accessions of wild and cultivated sorghum

entities.name: POPULATION STRUCTURE
entities.description: The genetic composition and distribution of alleles within the sorghum germplasm

entities.name: SSR MARKERS
entities.description: Simple Sequence Repeat marker loci used for genotyping and determining population structure

entities.name: LINKAGE GROUPS
entities.description: The 10 groups of the genome of cultivated Sorghum bicolor where markers are distributed

entities.name: GENOME
entities.description: The complete set of genetic material of Sorghum bicolor

entities.name: REFERENCE GERMPLASM SET
entities.description: A curated set of sorghum accessions developed for allele mining and linkage disequilibrium mapping

entities.name: ALLELE MINING
entities.description: The process of searching for specific alleles within a germplasm set

entities.name: LINKAGE DISEQUILIBRIUM MAPPING
entities.description: A method used to map traits based on the non-random association of alleles

entities.name: GENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of determining the genetic makeup of the germplasm set

relationships.description: Genotyping was performed on the composite germplasm set
The composite germplasm set consists of wild and cultivated sorghum accessions
SSR markers were used to determine the population structure
SSR markers are distributed across the genome
SSR markers are distributed across 10 linkage groups
Information on population structure was used to develop the reference germplasm set
The reference germplasm set is composed of sorghum
The reference germplasm set is used for allele mining
The reference germplasm set is used for linkage disequilibrium mapping
Additional SSR markers were used to validate the population structure of the reference germplasm set
